name: Mexican Shepard Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 package taco seasoning mix (Old El Paso brand)
1 block Cracker Barrel Extra Sharp Cheese, grated
water, as specified in the taco seasoning instructions
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Spray a 9x13 inch pan with Pam cooking spray.
3. Peel and quarter 10 medium potatoes. Place them in salted water. Bring to a boil and cook until fork-tender. Drain off excess water.
4. Add a stick of butter to the pot with the cooked potatoes and let melt.
5. Season the mashed potatoes with salt and pepper to taste.
6. Beat the potato mixture by hand or using a mixer. Gradually add 1/3 cup milk while beating, adjusting as needed for a creamy (but not runny) consistency.
7. Set aside.
8. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, cook the ground beef with a little salt and pepper to taste until browned.
9. Add chopped onion to the meat and continue cooking until well combined.
10. When the meat and onions are cooked, add the taco seasoning mix and water specified in the instructions (Old El Paso brand called for 1/3 cup). Reduce heat and simmer for about 10 minutes.
11. Add corn and salsa, then simmer for an additional 5 minutes to blend the flavors.
12. Assemble by spreading the meat mixture evenly over the bottom of the prepared pan.
13. Sprinkle a little grated cheese over the meat mixture.
14. Spread the mashed potatoes evenly over the meat mixture.
15. Sprinkle the remaining grated cheese over the potatoes.
16. Place in the oven and bake for 35 to 40 minutes, or until the cheese is fully melted and the dish turns golden brown and bubbly.
